posts.text:
What forces are driving the ideological shift within America’s elite universities?

Join us April 13 for a conversation with @EliseStefanik on her new book Poisoned Ivies—and the stakes for academic freedom, intellectual diversity, and our civic culture.

MI's @PolicyEngineer on New York's current fiscal situation:

"More than half of the state budget goes to just two areas—Medicaid and school aid. Those are both areas where the state, historically, has done a terrible job making sure that it gets a good return on its investments."

Video

We’re excited to announce our new Immigration Calculator by @DanielDiMartino.

The tool allows users to build a custom immigration reform agenda using 48 policy options or explore seven pre-designed proposals, with projections for GDP, population, and the national debt.
Watch the entire episode and subscribe: piped.video/watch?v=vYMFcjC-…

"Our view was that cities are crucibles of innovation, economic growth, platforms for opportunities so people can rise in life." - @BrianAcity on the @CityJournal Podcast.

Video

"It's just devastating that so much ugliness, violence, and sadness can be directed at what is a house of worship but also a preschool housing a bunch of little American Jewish boys and girls." - @Jesse_Leg on the Michigan synagogue attack.

Video

MI's @Rafa_Mangual on @BigBenShowFox on the attack outside Gracie Mansion:
 
“When you have so many of these flags attached to a single event, it cannot be policed just by precinct officers and community affairs. What you really need is some kind of counterterrorism element.”

Video
Watch the entire @CityJournal Podcast episode and subscribe: piped.video/vYMFcjC-rag?si=slis…

"This corrupting influence of technology on teaching and on education needs to be addressed.”  - @BrianAcity.

Video
Read his entire piece in @CityJournal: city-journal.org/article/new…

The failed attack at Gracie Mansion highlights the necessity of stronger public safety protocols in New York City.

MI's @Rafa_Mangual explains why federal prosecution is critical for keeping the city secure:

Video
For the latest from MI and @CityJournal, subscribe to our newsletter: manhattan.institute/subscrib…

NYC Faces Rising Extremist Threats

New York could face future extremist attacks, especially amid the war on Iran. MI’s @JMfreespeech reports in @CityJournal that the NYPD is monitoring streets, subways, bridges, and key infrastructure, using drones to support security efforts. bit.ly/3MZl0Zt

NYC School Safety Underreported

New York narrowed rules for reporting school violence, so serious incidents now appear far lower. Families can’t exercise their federal right to transfer kids from “persistently dangerous” schools, even though real safety risks persist, argues @neetu_arnold in @nypost. bit.ly/40N1RNH

Public Safety Under Strain

Saturday’s attack shows issues in Mayor Mamdani’s relationship with the NYPD. As @Rafa_Mangual writes in @CityJournal, New Yorkers can’t rely on luck—large gatherings need adequate police protection against terrorists and violent actors.  bit.ly/4sz4uOS

NYC Mayor and Saturday’s Attack

As NYC’s first Muslim mayor, Zohran Mamdani could speak out against Islamic extremism and promote unity. But by delaying acknowledgment of Saturday’s attack and ignoring the extremist ideology behind it, he failed that test, MI president @reihan writes in @TheFP. bit.ly/4bbET7m
